findings.description: The pitfall block states 'Running the first DFS on G instead of G^R' will cause failure, but Kosaraju's algorithm can equivalently be stated as: run DFS on G first to get post-order, then run DFS on G^R in decreasing post-order — this is a valid alternative formulation, so claiming the first DFS must be on G^R (and that doing it on G 'will fail') is a factual error; the algorithm works correctly either way as long as the second pass uses the transposed graph.
findings.suggested_fix: Restate the pitfall as: 'Running both DFS passes on the same graph (either both on G or both on G^R) without transposing for one pass will cause failure; the two passes must use G and G^R respectively.'
